stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 investment portfolio options. The probability of favorable market conditions is uncertain, estimated between 34% and 44%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: bonds: $159K in good market conditions, $58K in bad market conditions. index fund: $240K in good market conditions, $106K in bad market conditions. tech stocks: $293K in good market conditions, $106K in bad market conditions. real estate: $135K in good market conditions, $31K in bad market conditions. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.39: best option is tech stocks with EV=$178.9K. tech stocks is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [169.6, 188.3].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
tech stocks is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
